meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass: `make` runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. NEVER commit with failing checks. Pipe long output through `tail -200` to avoid context window exhaustion.
Rule of Two is MANDATORY before every commit: two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews of the exact same diff. Context-contamination resets to 0. Use strict-review-gate skill protocol EXACTLY.
Each task follows: work → check (make) → Rule of Two (strict-review-gate) → Commit (git-commit skill). Cycles are mandatory on failure until task is ACTUALLY COMPLETE AND CORRECT.

JS runs in Goja event loop — ALL blocking operations freeze the BubbleTea UI. Never call synchronous exec.execv() in update() or in async Promise continuations that run on the Goja microtask queue. Use exec.spawn() + await for all subprocess calls, or tea.tick() to yield between steps.
super_document_script.js is the REFERENCE implementation for viewport/scrollbar/mouse/keyboard patterns. ALL TUI code must follow its patterns exactly.
internal/termmux must remain clean of pr-split-specific logic. It will be extracted to its own module. Only general-purpose terminal multiplexer functionality belongs there.

The Mux VTerm captures ALL child output via teeLoop. Mux.ChildExitOutput() → vtm.String() (plain text). Mux.ChildScreen() → vtm.RenderFullScreen() (ANSI). CaptureSession has both output() and screen() (ANSI). Always use ANSI methods for visual rendering and plain text methods for assertions/diagnostics.

For the love of god DO NOT use exec to try and run GUI applications or anything that you need to see the output of. STOP BEING A MORON. That out of the way, you need to obey: exec.spawn() returns a Promise-based ChildHandle with stdout/stderr ReadableStreams and await-able wait(). This is the REQUIRED interface for non-blocking subprocess operations in async JS contexts. exec.execv() is ONLY acceptable in REPL mode or pre-BubbleTea contexts and ONLY if it is a short operation.

tuiMux is the global Mux instance connecting the TUI to the Claude child process. It is created Go-side (pr_split.go) and injected as a JS global. pollClaudeScreenshot() reads tuiMux.childScreen() for ANSI. writeToChild() sends keyboard input. These MUST be connected end-to-end for the Claude pane to function.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are sequential. Complete one fully (including running all relevant checks, then Rule of Two, iterate, then commit) before starting the next. Each task is self-contained. Break work into manageable units.
mandatoryDirectives.claudeIntegrationMandate: The core value proposition of osm pr-split is DEEP Claude integration with an interactive, muxed terminal pane embedded in the TUI. Every wizard phase must surface Claude's capabilities: prompting, classification, plan review feedback, error diagnosis, conflict resolution. The Claude pane must be ANSI-rendered, accept keyboard input when focused, and be switchable between screenshot mode and full passthrough.
mandatoryDirectives.eventLoopMandate: The Goja event loop processes microtasks (Promise continuations) after each _updateFn call. ANY blocking call in a Promise continuation freezes the UI until the call returns. All subprocess calls in async contexts MUST use exec.spawn() + await. Heuristic tick handlers MUST yield between operations via tea.tick().
mandatoryDirectives.integrationTestMandate: Real-Claude integration tests MUST validate all Claude interaction flows end-to-end. Run via `make integration-test-prsplit`. These tests spawn real Claude processes, exercise muxed terminal pane rendering, verify ANSI output, and test keyboard input forwarding. They are the ultimate proof the system works.

statusSection.goalState: A fully functional, production-quality pr-split TUI wizard with: (1) ANSI-rendered interactive Claude terminal pane with keyboard forwarding WORKING END-TO-END, (2) non-blocking async operations via exec.spawn throughout ALL TUI paths (heuristic AND auto), (3) proper viewport/scrollbar on all screens, (4) working keyboard+mouse navigation matching super-document patterns including in split-view mode, (5) real Claude integration with back-and-forth interaction in EVERY wizard phase, (6) embedded terminal output as BubbleTea component via termmux Mux with live ANSI rendering, (7) proper signal handling, (8) working error resolution flow with Claude diagnostics, (9) post-split verification with live CaptureSession output, (10) correct colors/layout with WCAG AA compliance, (11) comprehensive test coverage including real-Claude integration tests and VTerm observation tests, (12) exhaustive documentation of flows, architecture, and interactive patterns, (13) process output visible during all long-running operations via muxed CaptureSession panes.

sequentialTasks.task: Unblock Event Loop in Config & Verify Phases
sequentialTasks.description: Converted all blocking subprocess calls in async code paths to non-blocking exec.spawn() with async ReadableStream consumption. ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn()/restart() use resolveAsync() instead of blocking resolve(). verifySplitAsync() uses exec.spawn('sh', ...) instead of exec.execStream(). Pipeline automatedSplit() both resolve() call sites converted. Added isAvailableAsync(). All 39+ test mocks updated. Commit: 917915fa.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Config UI Interactivity and Alignment
sequentialTasks.description: Implemented inline config field editing for maxFiles (digit-only), branchPrefix, verifyCommand with keyboard interceptor (Enter/Esc/Backspace/Ctrl+U). Added dry-run checkbox toggle. Focus system extended with dynamic element IDs (field/checkbox types) exposed when showAdvanced=true. j/k navigation on CONFIG screen. Mouse click-to-edit fields, click-outside-blur, click-toggle dry run. State cleanup in ALL exit paths (toggle collapse, handleBack, handleNext, mouse blur). Column-aligned rendering with focus arrows and edit-mode cursor. focusIndex clamped on element removal. Initial state explicitly initialized.

sequentialTasks.task: Strengthen pipeline integration tests with robust assertions and cleanup ordering
sequentialTasks.description: Added 5 new pipeline-level integration tests (T106-T110) and strengthened 4 existing subcommand tests. T106: TestIntegration_FileContentsOnSplitBranches — byte-for-byte content verification on split branches. T107: TestIntegration_SingleFileFeature — single-file edge case produces exactly 1 split. T108: TestIntegration_MultiCommitFeature — cumulative diff across multiple commits preserved. T109: TestIntegration_EmptyFeatureBranch — graceful early exit on empty diff. T110: TestIntegration_ExecuteEquivalenceCleanupRoundTrip — full lifecycle: plan→execute→equiv→cleanup with postcondition assertions per step. Subcommand tests: execute verifies real git branches, equivalence rejects differ/not-equivalent (no vacuous pass), verify runs with 'true' command after execute, cleanup after_execute_removes_branches. ALL tests use chdirTestPipeline for correct LIFO cleanup ordering. T108 passes dir override to loadPrSplitEngine. Files: internal/command/pr_split_local_integration_test.go, internal/command/pr_split_tui_subcommands_test.go.
acceptanceCriteria: T106 verifies byte-identical file contents on split branches vs feature originals
T107 produces exactly 1 split branch for single-file feature
T108 handles multi-commit feature branch (3 commits, 6 files) with correct cumulative diff splitting
T109 gracefully exits on empty feature branch with no split branches created
T110 full lifecycle round-trip passes: plan→execute→equiv→cleanup with no split branches remaining
All chdir-based tests use chdirTestPipeline for correct LIFO cleanup ordering
Equivalence assertions reject vacuous passes (require 'equivalent', reject 'differ'/'not equivalent')
All 14 targeted tests pass via make test-prsplit-new

sequentialTasks.task: Implement actual-binary E2E tests proving osm pr-split works end-to-end
sequentialTasks.description: Added production code fix: non-interactive batch command dispatch path in pr_split.go Execute(). When interactive=false and testMode=false, positional args are dispatched as TUI commands via TUIManager.ExecuteCommand (batch mode) with nil guard, or if no args, starts a go-prompt REPL (for PTY-based observation tests). Created 7 binary E2E test functions + 1 assertion helper in pr_split_binary_e2e_test.go (//go:build unix): (1) TestBinaryE2E_HeuristicBatchRun — full 'run' command via subprocess, verifies exit 0, pipeline markers in output, 4+ split branches created with tree hash equivalence. (2) TestBinaryE2E_IndividualCommandsBatch — dispatches analyze/group/plan/execute as separate positional args in one invocation, proves in-process state carries through TUIManager. (3) TestBinaryE2E_FullPipelineWithCleanup — all 6 commands in single invocation (analyze→group→plan→execute→equivalence→cleanup), verifies branches are deleted after cleanup. (4) TestBinaryE2E_DryRunNoExecution — --dry-run flag shows plan preview (dry-run-specific markers only), no branches created. (5) TestBinaryE2E_EmptyDiff — empty feature branch produces no splits, detects panics vs graceful exits. (6) TestBinaryE2E_InvalidRepo — non-git directory produces meaningful error message. (7) TestBinaryE2E_HelpOutput — 'osm help' lists pr-split command.
acceptanceCriteria: Production code: non-interactive batch dispatch in pr_split.go handles positional args via TUIManager.ExecuteCommand
Production code: REPL fallback when no positional args for PTY observation tests
TestBinaryE2E_HeuristicBatchRun: binary exits 0 with 'Split executed/completed' + 'Done in' + equivalence markers + 2+ split branches

TestBinaryE2E_EmptyDiff: empty feature produces no splits, graceful exit
TestBinaryE2E_InvalidRepo: meaningful error message about missing git repository

sequentialTasks.task: Claude inline feedback during PLAN_GENERATION — detect questions, send answers
sequentialTasks.description: During PLAN_GENERATION with auto strategy, Claude may ask clarification questions (visible in terminal output). Enhance: (a) In handleAutoSplitPoll, detect when Claude's screen (s.claudeScreen) contains question markers (?, '>', 'please', 'clarif', 'would you') combined with idle state (no output for >2s via tuiMux.lastActivityMs()). (b) When detected, show inline prompt at bottom of analysis screen: 'Claude asks: [last line]. Type response:'. (c) User types response, Enter sends via tuiMux.writeToChild(response + '\r'). (d) Claude pane auto-scrolls. (e) Track conversation in s.claudeConversations[] for history. (f) Keyboard shortcut (Enter or Ctrl+R when question detected) to focus input. (g) Register zone.mark() IDs for the inline prompt input field and submit button. (h) Create 20+ representative Claude output snapshots as test fixtures for question-detection validation.

sequentialTasks.task: Claude context injection in ERROR_RESOLUTION with diagnostic info
sequentialTasks.description: When entering ERROR_RESOLUTION, Claude should receive diagnostic context automatically. Steps: (a) On transition to ERROR_RESOLUTION, if Claude is alive: compile error context (error message, stack trace, affected branch, recent git log, build output snapshot). (b) Send to Claude via sendToHandle as structured prompt. (c) Wait for Claude diagnosis via waitForLogged ('errorDiagnosis' tool). (d) Display Claude's diagnosis inline on ERROR_RESOLUTION screen. (e) If Claude suggests fix commands, offer 'Apply Fix' button. (f) Apply Fix: run commands via exec.spawn (non-blocking), show CaptureSession output, re-verify. (g) Fallback to manual if Claude unavailable. (h) Register zone.mark() IDs for 'Apply Fix' button and diagnosis display area.

sequentialTasks.task: Session persistence — wizard state checkpoint and resume after crash/cancel
sequentialTasks.description: Checkpoint system: (a) Save state on every wizard state transition (JSON to ~/.config/osm/pr-split-session.json). (b) On startup, check for session file → offer 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh'. (c) Persist the COMPLETED RESULTS of async operations and the current step index — do NOT serialize in-flight Promises (they serialize to {}). On resume, skip completed steps and re-launch any incomplete async operation from the last checkpoint step-progress state. State includes: wizard state, plan cache, analysis cache, groups cache, execution step index and per-branch results, error context, Claude session info. (d) Delete on successful completion. (e) Session lock file (check PID). (f) Handle stale locks.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel→restart offers 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh' options
Crash→restart offers 'Resume' or 'Start Fresh' options
Successful completion deletes session file at ~/.config/osm/pr-split-session.json
'Start Fresh' clears all persisted state
Session lock file prevents concurrent instances (checks PID)
Stale lock files (dead PID) handled gracefully
Step-progress state (not in-flight Promises) properly serialized; resume re-enters at correct wizard step

continuousVerification.description: Execute `make` which runs build, lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and test with race detection. ALL must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu, windows, macos). Use `make test` for fast feedback, `make` for full verification before commit.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Verify coverage for new code using `make cover`. Target >=90% for TUI functions, 100% for state machine transitions.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ALL checks must pass. NEVER commit with failing checks.
Rule of Two MANDATORY before every commit. Two contiguous issue-free reviews of the exact same diff.
JS runs in Goja event loop. Never call exec.execv() in update() or Promise continuations. Use exec.spawn() + await. Use tea.tick() to yield.
Claude pane MUST use ANSI rendering via Mux.ChildScreen()/vtm.RenderFullScreen(). screenshot() is for diagnostics/assertions only.
Interactive Claude keyboard input forwarding via Mux.WriteToChild() when Claude pane is focused. Reserve Ctrl+L/Tab/Ctrl+C/Ctrl+]/F1/? for wizard control.

tuiMux bootstrap MUST be complete: Go creates Mux → injects as JS global → pipeline spawns Claude → attaches to Mux → poll/render cycle reads childScreen(). Any break in this chain = dead Claude pane.
